hypothesis: If the Force Delta metric captures genuine physical inconsistency in the learned potential energy surface, then incorporating it as a regularization term during fine-tuning of direct-force pre-trained models—penalizing the magnitude of U∆across training configurations—will simultaneously reduce the non-conservativity of the force field and improve generalization to out-of-distribution structures, measurable as a statistically significant reduction in true force error on held-out adversarial test sets compared to models fine-tuned without this physics-informed regularization.
